info.name: GitHub API - 1. Basic (no Auth)
info.description:
### What is GitHub?

[GitHub](https://github.com/) is a popular code version control platform that over 73 million developers worldwide trust to organize their code bases.

While you can interact with GitHub by clicking around their website, you can also interact with GitHub programmatically using their API.

This collection shows just a few of the many actions you can perform using the [GitHub REST API](https://docs.github.com/en/rest).

### What is a repo?

Short for "repository", a repo is simply a place where code is stored. You can think of a repo like a project folder.

response.name: search repos llama

header.value: ETag, Link, Location, Retry-After, X-GitHub-OTP, X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Used, X-RateLimit-Resource, X-RateLimit-Reset, X-OAuth-Scopes, X-Accepted-OAuth-Scopes, X-Poll-Interval, X-GitHub-Media-Type, X-GitHub-SSO, X-GitHub-Request-Id, Deprecation, Sunset

variable.description: username or organization name you would like information about

request.description:
Get information about the given user. 

More detailed information is given if the requested user is the currently authorized user.

variable.description: username or organization name whose repos you would like to see

request.description: Lists all public repos for a given user
